title: Overview of supported structures
text: 4 basic types scalars JSON: boolean, integer, float, string. R really scalars vectors length 1. R vectors without JSON equivalent also supported long : vector vctrs definition size one, .e. vctrs::vec_size(x) 1. Examples Date POSIXct. general scalar can parsed tib_scalar(). special functions common types: tib_lgl() tib_int() tib_dbl() tib_chr() tib_date() tib_chr_date() parse dates encoded string.

title: Overview of supported structures
text: homogeneous JSON array array scalars scalar type. R correspond logical(), integer(), double() character() vector. scalars, vector types also supported long vector vctrs definition. can parsed tib_vector(). scalars, shortcuts common types, e.g. tib_lgl_vec().
code: [true, null, false] [1, null, 3] [1.5, null, 3.5] ["a", null, "c"] c(TRUE, NA, FALSE) c(1L, NA, 2L) c(1.5, NA, 2.5) c("a", NA, "c")

title: Overview of supported structures
text: Empty lists list() special case. might appear parsing empty JSON array. default supported produce error. Use vector_allows_empty_list = TRUE tspec_*() converted empty vector instead.
code: x_json <- '[   {"a": [1, 2]},   {"a": []} ]'  x <- jsonlite::fromJSON(x_json, simplifyDataFrame = FALSE) str(x) #> List of 2 #>  $ :List of 1 #>   ..$ a: int [1:2] 1 2 #>  $ :List of 1 #>   ..$ a: list() tibblify(x, tspec_df(tib_int_vec("a"))) #> Error in `tibblify()`: #> ! Problem while tibblifying `x[[2]]$a` #> Caused by error: #> ! Can't convert `<list>` <list> to <integer>. tibblify(x, tspec_df(tib_int_vec("a"), .vector_allows_empty_list = TRUE))$a #> <list_of<integer>[2]> #> [[1]] #> [1] 1 2 #>  #> [[2]] #> integer(0)

title: Overview of supported structures
text: using jsonlite::fromJSON(simplifyVector = FALSE) parse JSON R object one get R vectors homogeneous lists scalars. default parsed tib_vector(). Use .input_form = "scalar_list" tib_vector() parse :
code: x_json <- '[   {"a": [1, 2]},   {"a": [1, 2, 3]} ]'  x <- jsonlite::fromJSON(x_json, simplifyVector = FALSE) str(x) #> List of 2 #>  $ :List of 1 #>   ..$ a:List of 2 #>   .. ..$ : int 1 #>   .. ..$ : int 2 #>  $ :List of 1 #>   ..$ a:List of 3 #>   .. ..$ : int 1 #>   .. ..$ : int 2 #>   .. ..$ : int 3 tibblify(x, tspec_df(tib_int_vec("a"))) #> Error in `tibblify()`: #> ! Problem while tibblifying `x[[1]]$a` #> Caused by error: #> ! Can't convert `<list>` <list> to <integer>. tibblify(x, tspec_df(tib_int_vec("a", .input_form = "scalar_list")))$a #> <list_of<integer>[2]> #> [[1]] #> [1] 1 2 #>  #> [[2]] #> [1] 1 2 3

title: Overview of supported structures
text: Sometimes vectors encoded objects JSON. Use .input_form = "object" tib_vector() parse . store names use .names_to .values_to arguments.
code: x_json <- '[   {"a": {"x": 1, "y": 2}},   {"a": {"a": 1, "b": 2, "b": 3}} ]'  x <- jsonlite::fromJSON(x_json, simplifyVector = FALSE) str(x) #> List of 2 #>  $ :List of 1 #>   ..$ a:List of 2 #>   .. ..$ x: int 1 #>   .. ..$ y: int 2 #>  $ :List of 1 #>   ..$ a:List of 3 #>   .. ..$ a: int 1 #>   .. ..$ b: int 2 #>   .. ..$ b: int 3 spec <- tspec_df(   tib_int_vec(     "a",     .input_form = "object",     .names_to = "name",     .values_to = "value"   ) )  tibblify(x, spec)$a[[1]] #> # A tibble: 2 × 2 #>   name  value #>   <chr> <int> #> 1 x         1 #> 2 y         2 tibblify(x, spec)$a[[2]] #> # A tibble: 3 × 2 #>   name  value #>   <chr> <int> #> 1 a         1 #> 2 b         2 #> 3 b         3

title: Overview of supported structures
text: R equivalent JSON object named list names fulfill requirements vctrs::vec_as_names(repair = "check_unique"). can parsed tib_row(). example:
code: {   "a": 1,   "b": true } x <- list(   a = 1,   b = TRUE ) x <- list(   list(row = list(a = 1, b = TRUE)),   list(row = list(a = 2, b = FALSE)) )  spec <- tspec_df(   tib_row(     "row",     tib_int("a"),     tib_lgl("b")   ) )  tibblify(x, spec) #> # A tibble: 2 × 1 #>   row$a $b    #>   <int> <lgl> #> 1     1 TRUE  #> 2     2 FALSE

code: [   {"a": 1, "b": true},   {"b": 2, "b": false} ] x <- list(   list(a = 1, b = TRUE),   list(a = 2, b = FALSE) )

title: Overview of supported structures
text: JSON can also store named lists objects. JSON represented objects element object. also parsed tib_df(), can parse names extra column via .names_to argument:
code: {   "object1": {"a": 1, "b": true},   "object2": {"b": 2, "b": false} } x <- list(   object1 = list(a = 1, b = TRUE),   object2 = list(a = 2, b = FALSE) ) x_json <- '[ {   "df": {     "object1": {"a": 1, "b": true},     "object2": {"a": 2, "b": false}   } }]'  x <- jsonlite::fromJSON(x_json, simplifyDataFrame = FALSE)  spec <- tspec_df(   tib_df(     "df",     tib_int("a"),     tib_lgl("b"),     .names_to = "name"   ) )  tibblify(x, spec)$df #> <list_of< #>   tbl_df< #>     name: character #>     a   : integer #>     b   : logical #>   > #> >[1]> #> [[1]] #> # A tibble: 2 × 3 #>   name        a b     #>   <chr>   <int> <lgl> #> 1 object1     1 TRUE  #> 2 object2     2 FALSE

title: Overview of supported structures
text: column-major format also supported. Parse using .input_form = "colmajor" tspec_*(). roughly equivalent tibble::as_tibble(x).
code: {   "a": [1, 2],   "b": [true, false] } x <- list(   a = c(1, 2),   b = c(TRUE, FALSE) ) df_spec <- tspec_df(   tib_int("a"),   tib_lgl("b"),   .input_form = "colmajor" )  tibblify(x, df_spec) #> # A tibble: 2 × 2 #>       a b     #>   <int> <lgl> #> 1     1 TRUE  #> 2     2 FALSE

title: tibblify
text: Let’s start gh_users, list containing information four GitHub users. ’ll select subset columns keep example relatively simple. can quickly rectangle gh_users_small tibblify(). can now look specification tibblify() used rectangling. interested fields can easily adapt specification.
code: gh_users_small <- purrr::map(gh_users, ~ .x[c("followers", "login", "url", "name", "location", "email", "public_gists")])  names(gh_users_small[[1]]) #> [1] "followers"    "login"        "url"          "name"         "location"     #> [6] "email"        "public_gists" tibblify(gh_users_small) #> The spec contains 1 unspecified field: #> • email #> # A tibble: 4 × 7 #>   followers login      url                    name  location email  public_gists #>       <int> <chr>      <chr>                  <chr> <chr>    <list>        <int> #> 1       780 jennybc    https://api.github.co… Jenn… Vancouv… <NULL>           54 #> 2      3958 jtleek     https://api.github.co… Jeff… Baltimo… <NULL>           12 #> 3       115 juliasilge https://api.github.co… Juli… Salt La… <NULL>            4 #> 4       213 leeper     https://api.github.co… Thom… London,… <NULL>           46 guess_tspec(gh_users_small) #> The spec contains 1 unspecified field: #> • email #> tspec_df( #>   tib_int("followers"), #>   tib_chr("login"), #>   tib_chr("url"), #>   tib_chr("name"), #>   tib_chr("location"), #>   tib_unspecified("email"), #>   tib_int("public_gists"), #> ) spec <- tspec_df(   login_name = tib_chr("login"),   tib_chr("name"),   tib_int("public_gists") )  tibblify(gh_users_small, spec) #> # A tibble: 4 × 3 #>   login_name name                   public_gists #>   <chr>      <chr>                         <int> #> 1 jennybc    Jennifer (Jenny) Bryan           54 #> 2 jtleek     Jeff L.                          12 #> 3 juliasilge Julia Silge                       4 #> 4 leeper     Thomas J. title: tibblify
text: refer lists like gh_users_small collections, element list object. Objects collections typical input tibblify(). Basically, object something can converted one-row tibble. boils condition names object: object must names (names attribute must NULL). Every element must named (name can NA ""). names must unique. words, names must fulfill vctrs::vec_as_names(names, repair = "check_unique"). name-value pairs object fields. example list(x = 1, y = "") object fields (x, 1) (y, "") list(1, z = 3) object fully named. collection list similar objects fields can become columns tibble.

title: tibblify
text: Providing explicit specification several advantages: can ensure type shape stability resulting tibble automated scripts. can give columns names different names source collection. can parse fields need (ignore others). can specify happens value missing. create specification collection, use tspec_df(). Describe columns output tibble tib_*() functions. tib_*() functions describe path field extract output type field. Throughout functions, use concept ptype (prototype) used {vctrs} package (see vignette("type-size", package = "vctrs")). five main types tib_*() functions: tib_scalar(ptype): length-one vector type ptype. result column type. tib_vector(ptype): vector arbitrary length type ptype. result list column, row containing vector type. tib_variant(): vector arbitrary length type. result list column, row containing list. rarely need function. tib_row(...): object fields .... result tibble column, row containing specified one-row tibble. tib_df(...): collection objects fields .... result tibble column, row containing tibble. also two tib_*() functions special cases: tib_recursive(): collection objects can collections structure, directory tree. result tibble column, row containing tibble structure (NULL reached, terminating recursion). tib_unspecified(): unspecified field type shape known advance. unspecified argument tibblify() controls fields handled. convenience shortcuts tib_scalar() tib_vector() common prototypes: logical(): tib_lgl() tib_lgl_vec() integer(): tib_int() tib_int_vec() double(): tib_dbl() tib_dbl_vec() character(): tib_chr() tib_chr_vec() Date: tib_date() tib_date_vec() Date encoded character: tib_chr_date() tib_chr_date_vec()

title: tibblify
text: Scalar elements common case result normal vector column. tib_scalar(), can also provide prototype column types included shortcuts. example, let’s say list durations (objects class “difftime”). Use tib_scalar() vctrs::new_duration() specify duration ptype.
code: tibblify(   list(     list(id = 1, name = "Peter"),     list(id = 2, name = "Lilly")   ),   tspec_df(     tib_int("id"),     tib_chr("name")   ) ) #> # A tibble: 2 × 2 #>      id name  #>   <int> <chr> #> 1     1 Peter #> 2     2 Lilly x <- list(   list(id = 1, duration = vctrs::new_duration(100)),   list(id = 2, duration = vctrs::new_duration(200)) ) x #> [[1]] #> [[1]]$id #> [1] 1 #>  #> [[1]]$duration #> Time difference of 100 secs #>  #>  #> [[2]] #> [[2]]$id #> [1] 2 #>  #> [[2]]$duration #> Time difference of 200 secs tibblify(   x,   tspec_df(     tib_int("id"),     tib_scalar("duration", .ptype = vctrs::new_duration())   ) ) #> # A tibble: 2 × 2 #>      id duration #>   <int> <drtn>   #> 1     1 100 secs #> 2     2 200 secs

title: tibblify
text: Objects usually fields always exist optional. default tib_*() requires field exists. can mark field optional argument .required = FALSE. can specify value use .fill argument.
code: x <- list(   list(x = 1, y = "a"),   list(x = 2) )  spec <- tspec_df(   x = tib_int("x"),   y = tib_chr("y") )  tibblify(x, spec) #> Error in `tibblify()`: #> ! Field y is required but does not exist in `x[[2]]`. #> ℹ Use `required = FALSE` if the field is optional. spec <- tspec_df(   x = tib_int("x"),   y = tib_chr("y", .required = FALSE) )  tibblify(x, spec) #> # A tibble: 2 × 2 #>       x y     #>   <int> <chr> #> 1     1 a     #> 2     2 NA spec <- tspec_df(   x = tib_int("x"),   y = tib_chr("y", .required = FALSE, .fill = "missing") )  tibblify(x, spec) #> # A tibble: 2 × 2 #>       x y       #>   <int> <chr>   #> 1     1 a       #> 2     2 missing

title: tibblify
text: rectangle single object two options: tspec_object() produces list, tspec_row() produces tibble one row. often makes sense convert objects list. example typical API response might something like . Use tspec_row() convert one-row tibble. access data one use api_output_df$data[[1]], nice. can use tspec_object() simplify situation. Now accessing data require extra subsetting step.
code: api_output <- list(   status = "success",   requested_at = "2021-10-26 09:17:12",   data = list(     list(x = 1),     list(x = 2)   ) ) row_spec <- tspec_row(   status = tib_chr("status"),   data = tib_df(     "data",     x = tib_int("x")   ) )  api_output_df <- tibblify(api_output, row_spec) api_output_df #> # A tibble: 1 × 2 #>   status                data #>   <chr>   <list<tibble[,1]>> #> 1 success            [2 × 1] object_spec <- tspec_object(   status = tib_chr("status"),   data = tib_df(     "data",     x = tib_int("x")   ) )  api_output_list <- tibblify(api_output, object_spec) api_output_list #> $status #> [1] "success" #>  #> $data #> # A tibble: 2 × 1 #>       x #>   <int> #> 1     1 #> 2     2 api_output_list$data # No [[1]] needed #> # A tibble: 2 × 1 #>       x #>   <int> #> 1     1 #> 2     2

title: Rectangle Nested Lists
text: illustrate tibblify() works, ’ll start list containing information four GitHub users. can rectangle gh_users_small automatically tibblify(): can avoid note unspecified field formally providing spec starting guess_tspec(): Learn vignette("tibblify").
code: library(tibblify)  gh_users_small <- purrr::map(gh_users, ~ .x[c("followers", "login", "url", "name", "location", "email", "public_gists")])  names(gh_users_small[[1]]) #> [1] "followers"    "login"        "url"          "name"         "location"     #> [6] "email"        "public_gists" tibblify(gh_users_small) #> The spec contains 1 unspecified field: #> • email #> # A tibble: 4 × 7 #>   followers login      url                    name  location email  public_gists #>       <int> <chr>      <chr>                  <chr> <chr>    <list>        <int> #> 1       780 jennybc    https://api.github.co… Jenn… Vancouv… <NULL>           54 #> 2      3958 jtleek     https://api.github.co… Jeff… Baltimo… <NULL>           12 #> 3       115 juliasilge https://api.github.co… Juli… Salt La… <NULL>            4 #> 4       213 leeper     https://api.github.co… Thom… London,… <NULL>           46 spec <- guess_tspec(gh_users_small, inform_unspecified = FALSE) # Drop the unused email specification. spec$fields$email <- NULL tibblify(gh_users_small, spec = spec) #> # A tibble: 4 × 6 #>   followers login      url                           name  location public_gists #>       <int> <chr>      <chr>                         <chr> <chr>           <int> #> 1       780 jennybc    https://api.github.com/users… Jenn… Vancouv…           54 #> 2      3958 jtleek     https://api.github.com/users… Jeff… Baltimo…           12 #> 3       115 juliasilge https://api.github.com/users… Juli… Salt La…            4 #> 4       213 leeper     https://api.github.com/users… Thom… London,…           46

title: Convert a data frame to a tree — nest_tree
text: Recursively nest data frame rows based parent-child relationships, defined id column parent column. Children become sub-tibbles parent rows. structure intended representing tree-like data, organizational charts, file systems, category trees, hierarchical relationships.

code: df <- tibble::tibble(   id = 1:5,   x = letters[1:5],   parent = c(NA, NA, 1L, 2L, 4L) ) df #> # A tibble: 5 × 3 #>      id x     parent #>   <int> <chr>  <int> #> 1     1 a         NA #> 2     2 b         NA #> 3     3 c          1 #> 4     4 d          2 #> 5     5 e          4  # Only the root elements are in the top-level tibble. out <- nest_tree(df, id, parent, "children") out #> # A tibble: 2 × 3 #>      id x     children         #>   <int> <chr> <list>           #> 1     1 a     <tibble [1 × 3]> #> 2     2 b     <tibble [1 × 3]>  # The children of each element are stored in the "children" column. out$children #> [[1]] #> # A tibble: 1 × 3 #>      id x     children #>   <int> <chr> <list>   #> 1     3 c     <NULL>   #>  #> [[2]] #> # A tibble: 1 × 3 #>      id x     children         #>   <int> <chr> <list>           #> 1     4 d     <tibble [1 × 3]> #>   # "d" (id 4) is a child of "b" (id 2), and "e" (id 5) is a child of "d" # (id 4). out$children[[2]]$children #> [[1]] #> # A tibble: 1 × 3 #>      id x     children #>   <int> <chr> <list>   #> 1     5 e     <NULL>   #>

title: Create a field specification — tib_spec
text: .key, key (character) path names field object. .ptype, ptype (vector(0)) prototype desired output type field. ... dots future extensions must empty. .required, required (logical(1)) Throw error field exist? .fill, fill (vector NULL) Optionally, value use field exist. .ptype_inner, ptype_inner (vector(0)) prototype input field. .transform, transform (function NULL) function apply whole vector casting .ptype_inner. .elt_transform, elt_transform (function NULL) function apply element casting .ptype_inner. .input_form, input_form (character(1)) structure input field. Can one : "vector": field vector, e.g. c(1, 2, 3). "scalar_list": field list scalars, e.g. list(1, 2, 3). "object": field named list scalars, e.g. list(= 1, b = 2, c = 3). .values_to, values_to (character(1) NULL) NULL (default), field converted .ptype vector. string provided, field converted tibble values go specified column. .names_to, names_to (character(1) NULL) inner names object. Can one : NULL: default. inner names field used. string: Use input form "object" "vector", .values_to string. inner names field populate specified column field's tibble. .format, format (character(1) NULL) Passed format argument .Date(). .children (character(1)) name field contains children. .children_to (character(1)) column name store children.

title: Create a field specification — tib_spec
text: tibblify field collector. specification can used tspec_df() another tspec_*() function specify process object.

title: Create a field specification — tib_spec
text: five families tib_*() functions: tib_scalar(.ptype): Cast instance field length-one vector type .ptype. Inside tspec_df(), results column specified .ptype. tib_vector(.ptype): Cast instance field arbitrary length vector type .ptype. Inside tspec_df(), results list column vectors specified .ptype. tib_variant(): Cast instance field list. Inside tspec_df(), results list column lists. tib_row(): Cast instance field 1-row tibble. Inside tspec_df(), results list column 1-row tibbles. tib_df(): Cast instance field tibble. Inside tspec_df(), results list column tibbles (can multiple rows). special shortcuts tib_scalar() tib_vector() common prototypes: logical(): tib_lgl() tib_lgl_vec() integer(): tib_int() tib_int_vec() double(): tib_dbl() tib_dbl_vec() character(): tib_chr() tib_chr_vec() Date: tib_date() tib_date_vec() , special shortcuts dates encoded character: tib_chr_date() tib_chr_date_vec(). two tib_*() functions special cases: tib_recursive(): Cast instance field tibble, within columns can contain sorts tibble, etc. Inside tspec_df(), results list column tibbles, row can contain tibble, etc. intended structures directory tree. tib_unspecified(): Tag field object unspecified. unspecified argument tibblify() controls fields handled. constructing specification manually (opposed using guess_tspec()), likely specify columns tib_variant(), leave spec entirely.

code: # List of Objects ----------------------------------------------------------- x <- list(   list(id = 1, name = "Tyrion Lannister"),   list(id = 2, name = "Victarion Greyjoy") ) tibblify(x) #> # A tibble: 2 × 2 #>      id name              #>   <dbl> <chr>             #> 1     1 Tyrion Lannister  #> 2     2 Victarion Greyjoy  # Provide a specification spec <- tspec_df(   id = tib_int("id"),   name = tib_chr("name") ) tibblify(x, spec) #> # A tibble: 2 × 2 #>      id name              #>   <int> <chr>             #> 1     1 Tyrion Lannister  #> 2     2 Victarion Greyjoy  # Object -------------------------------------------------------------------- # Provide a specification for a single object tibblify(x[[1]], tspec_object(spec)) #> $id #> [1] 1 #>  #> $name #> [1] "Tyrion Lannister" #>   # Recursive Trees ----------------------------------------------------------- x <- list(   list(     id = 1,     name = "a",     children = list(       list(id = 11, name = "aa"),       list(id = 12, name = "ab", children = list(         list(id = 121, name = "aba")       ))     )) ) spec <- tspec_recursive(   tib_int("id"),   tib_chr("name"),   .children = "children" ) out <- tibblify(x, spec) out #> # A tibble: 1 × 3 #>      id name  children         #>   <int> <chr> <list>           #> 1     1 a     <tibble [2 × 3]> out$children #> [[1]] #> # A tibble: 2 × 3 #>      id name  children         #>   <int> <chr> <list>           #> 1    11 aa    <NULL>           #> 2    12 ab    <tibble [1 × 3]> #>  out$children[[1]]$children[[2]] #> # A tibble: 1 × 3 #>      id name  children #>   <int> <chr> <list>   #> 1   121 aba   <NULL>

Please notify us (https://github.com/wranglezone/tibblify/issues) something changed worse unexpected way (#243). guess_tspec() variants guess_tspec_list() guess_tspec_object_list() now exported (along guess_tspec_df() guess_tspec_object(), already exported). guess_tspec() correctly guess format cases, can call variant directly think guess_tspec() dispatching incorrectly (#249). untibblify() now automatically uses tib_spec attribute present, tibblified objects can round-tripped without explicitly passing spec (#235). parse_openapi_spec() supports many fields works many APIs (#190, #200, @jonthegeek @mgirlich). underlying C implementation updated better comply R’s C API. also fixed various bugs update (#203, #204, #222). vignettes documentation functions updated clarity (#243). (roughly sorted “Breaking changes”, “Potential breaking changes”, “New features”, “Bug fixes”, “Documentation” 2026-04-10, left headers make easier add bullets development)

title: tibblify 0.3.1
text: CRAN release: 2024-01-11 New parse_openapi_spec() parse_openapi_schema() convert OpenAPI specification tibblify specification. Fix ptype tib_vector() inside tib_df(). New unpack_tspec() unpack elements tib_row() fields (#165). Improved printing lists parsed tspec_object(). Improved performance tspec() family. Improved guessing.

title: tibblify 0.3.0
text: CRAN release: 2022-11-16 column major format fields required. Fixed memory leak. tib_vector() now uses less memory faster. tspec_*(), tib_df(), tib_row() now discard NULL .... makes easier add field conditionally , example tspec_df((x) tib_int("")). tib_variant() tib_vector() give control transforming: transform now applied whole vector. new elt_transform argument applied every element. New tspec_recursive() tib_recursive() parse tree like structure, e.g. directory structure children.

title: tibblify 0.2.0
text: CRAN release: 2022-07-14 Major rewrite tibblify package lots benefits: tibblify() now implemented C therefore way faster. Support column major format. Support vectors scalar lists objects. Specification functions renamed lcols() tspec_df() new specs tspec_object() tspec_row() lcol_int() tib_int() etc tspec_df() gains argument .names_to store names recordlist column. Added untibblify() turn tibble nested list, .e. reverse action tibblify(). Added spec_combine() combine multiple specifications. Added argument unspecified tibblify() control handle unspecified fields. Many bugfixes.

title: tibblify 0.1.0
